extra_model_fields.priority_justification: Medium - changes increase verifier confidence and auditability without altering core decision logic; moderate chance of small formatting regressions which will be caught by the review steps.
verification_steps_for_reviewer: Open the revised proton_robustness.xlsx and inspect the Validation sheet and the sample formula bar screenshots.
Confirm formulas.txt matches the formula bar contents and that cell addresses in memo correspond to workbook cells.
Confirm that sensitivity parameters are present and that changing them updates sensitivity columns.
Confirm tie-break policy is documented and applied consistently in the memo's examples.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: formulas.txt attached, 3 screenshots of formula bar included, updated proton_robustness.xlsx saved with live formulas; verifier can confirm formulas without ambiguity.
planned_changes.description: Verify and document spreadsheet formulas: open proton_robustness.xlsx in a spreadsheet editor, confirm that distal_margin_mm, required_gap_mm, sensitivity, and remediation-effect columns contain live formulas. If programmatic parsing is not available to verifiers, export a formulas.txt mapping cell addresses to formula expressions and capture screenshots of the formula bar for representative rows (Inputs row, Audit/Validation row, remediation-effect row). Attach these to the revised attempt.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: memo preview shows complete decision table with no unmatched quotes and includes a top-level safety statement.
planned_changes.description: Correct and republish treatment_gate_memo.md: remove truncation/malformed characters, ensure decision table rows are complete, and rerun a full preview to confirm no truncation. Add a one-line safety header (e.g., 'Expert review required before clinical action').

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Memo contains cell-address links (e.g., Inputs!A2) and the workbook includes a Validation sheet with re-derivable checks and pass/fail flags.
planned_changes.description: Add explicit cell references and a Validation sheet: in the memo, add Inputs! cell addresses for each packet-cited input row and reference the Audit/Validation sheet cell addresses used for distal_margin_mm and required_gap_mm. In the spreadsheet, add a Validation sheet that reproduces the key checks and includes pass/fail flags and reproducibility notes.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Memo and spreadsheet contain explicit sensitivity parameters or justification and a documented tie-break policy. Sensitivity columns update correctly when parameters are changed.
planned_changes.description: Document sensitivity assumptions and tie-break policy: either parameterize the sensitivity deltas in the spreadsheet (top-row input cells for coverage_delta_mm and gap_delta_mm) or explicitly justify the chosen +/-1% and +/-1 mm values in the memo. Add a short tie-break rule (e.g., 'coverage >= threshold is considered pass; for equality of coverage==threshold, require OAR_gap > required_gap to pass') and apply it to the documented examples.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Spreadsheet contains pre/post remediation columns and memo cites their cell addresses; remediation arithmetic matches packet option deltas.
planned_changes.description: Add explicit pre/post remediation computed columns and reference them in the memo: create columns that show 'post_remediation_gap' and 'post_remediation_coverage' with formulas applying the packet-specified deltas; ensure these cells are referenced in the memo with cell addresses.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: final_message.txt lists the revised artifact filenames and briefly summarizes the auditability improvements.
planned_changes.description: Update final_message.txt to reference the revised artifacts and explain the changes made for verifier convenience.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline artifacts and memo are complete, correctly transcribe packet values, and apply the stated decision logic and remediation deltas. Grader and verifier reached a passing conclusion (final_score 1.0). Remaining verification gaps are procedural/auditability items: (1) inability to programmatically open and validate live formulas in proton_robustness.xlsx (openpyxl/xlsxwriter unavailable), (2) truncated/malformed snippet in the memo preview (unmatched quote at the end of the decision table), (3) the memo documents assumed sensitivity perturbations (+/-1% coverage, +/-1 mm gap) that were not specified by the original task and should be explicitly justified or parameterized, and (4) no explicit tie-break policy for equality-at-threshold cases (e.g., coverage == 97%). These are low-to-medium severity and can be resolved with focused, low-risk revisions to improve auditability and verifier confidence.
